weather.summary: Saturday: 14°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 12°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting opportunities for your 3-year-old! From Easter art trails to getting up close with animals, there's something to spark everyone's interest. I've made sure to include a mix of indoor and outdoor options to dodge any rain showers.

activities.title: Camden Market Easter Weekender
activities.description: A free Easter Weekender including an Easter art trail, face painting, and bonnet making. Soak up the atmosphere and street performances; costs only apply if you decide to eat or shop.
activities.whyItWorks: The art trail and face painting are great for a 3-year-old's creative side, plus the bustling market atmosphere is exciting.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Camden Road, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Can be crowded, so keep a close eye on little ones.

activities.title: Two weeks of Easter activities in Victoria Park
activities.description: Victoria Park hosts a fortnight of free, daily activities, including sports, fishing, and pond dipping. There's a break for the Easter weekend, but activities resume afterwards.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space to run around, plus a variety of activities to suit different interests. Pond dipping is a great hands-on experience.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes 277 and 425 go directly to Victoria Park.
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for a schedule of activities and booking details.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: The Young V&A (formerly the Museum of Childhood) is designed with children in mind, offering interactive galleries focusing on design, imagine and play. There is a Mini Museum for under 2s providing a sensory experience.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, dedicated under 2s area, and plenty to stimulate young minds.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Can get busy during peak hours, so plan accordingly.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm where children can see farm animals up close. Perfect for a short visit.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals are always a hit with toddlers, and the farm is small enough to not be overwhelming.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses go to Hackney Road. Check local routes.
transport.tip: Check for special events or feeding times.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small playground designed for toddlers, featuring a pond theme and wooden structures.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddler-friendly design, close to other attractions in the Olympic Park.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Spitalfields Market Free Easter Art Workshops
activities.description: Free Easter Art Workshops for all ages with LuluJoelle from 3rd-6th April.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and hands-on, suitable for all ages and indoors, which is perfect for the weather.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the Spitalfields Market website for workshop times.

activities.title: Two Weeks of Easter Activities
activities.description: The Discover Children's Story Centre offers two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: This venue offers immersive play spaces that will ignite the imagination of your three-year-old. The combination of storytelling, crafts, and singalongs is perfectly tailored for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford Station, then a short walk
transport.tip: Consider booking in advance, especially for popular times.

activities.title: Free events at Imagine
activities.description: The Southbank Centre offers various free events for families, including Giant Chalkboard, WordPlay for ages 0-2 and 3-5, and Architecture Explorers: Family Trail.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of creative and interactive activities, suitable for different age groups. Free and easily accessible.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. A short walk across the river.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for the daily schedule.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating exhibits, and family-friendly activities at this world-renowned museum. The Easter Family Festival runs from April 5 to April 20, with free activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs and interactive exhibits will capture a 3-year-old's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Arrive early to avoid long queues, especially during the Easter Family Festival.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works through interactive exhibits, including a space exploration gallery. The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for younger children to discover construction, water, light and sound through play.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits and dedicated children's areas make learning fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check out the Wonderlab for a hands-on experience (ticket required).

activities.title: London Zoo Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Wander past incredible wildlife as you search for six giant hidden Easter eggs. Each egg reveals a secret letter and encourages curious minds to work together to uncover the code hidden throughout the Zoo. Complete the trail to get a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals plus an Easter egg hunt equals toddler heaven.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria Line to Euston. Short bus ride to the Zoo.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance, arrive early to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children, with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool (seasonal), and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Large play area with a variety of activities, plus a city farm.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Russell Square. Short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry if accompanying a child.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A free zoo with a variety of animals, including birds, goats, and deer.
activities.whyItWorks: Free and manageable for a short visit with a toddler.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Gospel Oak, then the Overground to Golders Green. Short bus ride to the park.
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm for Easter crafts and animals
activities.description: From March 28 to April 12, Vauxhall City Farm is running free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts. Located in Zone 1, it is a must for families and animal-lovers, with a host of goats, sheep and smaller animals right on your doorstep.
activities.whyItWorks: With free Easter hunts, crafts and animals, this destination offers a wide range of toddler-friendly activities in one place.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria Line to Vauxhall Station.
transport.tip: Check the Vauxhall City Farm website for specific times and activities.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat. Families are invited into a storybook adventure through centuries of royal history.
activities.whyItWorks: The perfect playful treasure hunt. Lasting around 90 minutes and designed as a self-led journey, the experience is something worth remembering.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then a train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance, check train schedules.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Offers a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a short bus ride to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Check the Battersea Park website for events and activities.

activities.title: Mini Makers: Messy Play
activities.description: Messy play sessions for under 5s.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers love messy play, and this is a dedicated session.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Ealing Broadway.
transport.tip: Check the Pitzhanger Manor & Gallery website for times and booking information.

activities.title: Laaaunch!
activities.description: A multi-sensory space-themed show.
activities.whyItWorks: Space themes are visually stimulating for young children, and the multi-sensory aspect is perfect for babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the District Line to Wimbledon.
transport.tip: Check the Polka Theatre website for times and booking information.

activities.title: The Enormous Crocodile
activities.description: Musical based on the Roald Dahl book.
activities.whyItWorks: Roald Dahl stories are always a hit with kids, and this is an outdoor theatre experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Baker Street.
transport.tip: Check the Regent's Park Open Air Theatre website for times and booking information.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Children aged 8 and under are invited to discover a variety of illustrated animals nestled amongst the blooming habitats of the reserve, identify the letter on each one and complete the secret word in the activity booklet.
activities.whyItWorks: This engaging trail offers a combination of nature, discovery, and literacy, perfect for young children. The interactive element of finding animals and completing the secret word adds an extra layer of fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to St James Street Station, then a short walk
transport.tip: Check the Walthamstow Wetlands website for times and booking information.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers 2026
activities.description: A family Easter adventure in West London. Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: A brilliant Easter day out designed to create joyful spring memories for children and their grown-ups. Hobbledown Heath has indoor play areas, making Easter Eggsplorers a great option whatever the weather.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hounslow West
transport.tip: Check the Hobbledown Heath website for times and booking information.

activities.title: War Horse
activities.description: Based on Michael Morpurgo's heartbreaking novel, its breathtaking fusion of drama and innovative puppetry is a masterpiece of modern theatre. It has spent two decades conquering the world, winning accolades wherever it plays.
activities.whyItWorks: A visually stunning show with innovative puppetry, suitable for older children and adults.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. Short walk across the river.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, check travel times.

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: Welcome the world's most beloved bear to the stage! Pack your marmalade sandwiches and get down to the Savoy Theatre.
activities.whyItWorks: Paddington is a well-known character that most toddlers will recognise. A great family show for the half term.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. Short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, and bring marmalade sandwiches.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: Join Alice and tumble down the rabbit hole into the fantastical world of Wonderland.
activities.whyItWorks: This immersive play makes use of some of the more exciting and aesthetically pleasing works from its formidable collection, but genuinely feels like a 'proper' exhibition and not just a few bonus bits in a corner.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shepherds Bush, then a bus to Hammersmith.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, check travel times.
